frontmatter.name: Control Flow as Graph
frontmatter.shortDescription: Represent execution paths through a method or function as a graph
Catch blocks always get executed
Only the necessary part of a catch block executes
A condition node in a control-flow graph can have a single outgoing edge
If-else is equivalent to sequence of two ifs
If-then-else block is equivalent to sequence of two if-then blocks

expressibleInNms.how: CFG showing sequence of two ifs vs. an if-else

frontmatter.shortDescription: A return statement in the middle of a method doesn't return immediately
A return statement in the middle of a function doesn't return immediately
There is an elsif keyword for multi-way conditional statements

expressibleInNms.how: graph of if-else-if with both conditions

frontmatter.shortDescription: The else branch of an if-else statement always executes
The else branch of an if-then-else block always executes
The body of a for statement executes at most once
The body of an if statement executes repeatedly, as long as the condition holds
The body of an if-statement executes repeatedly, as long as the condition holds
Every if statement requires an else
In an intra-procedural control-flow graph, method call nodes point to the called method
For and while loops end when the condition becomes true
Blocks following an if without else will not execute if the condition is false
&& and || always evaluate both operands

expressibleInNms.how: graph of conditional involving short-circuit operator

frontmatter.shortDescription: and/or always evaluate both operands

expressibleInNms.how: graph of conditional involving short-circuit operator

frontmatter.shortDescription: Pre-increment in update part of for loop means increment before loop body

expressibleInNms.how: graph of for loop with pre-increment in update part

frontmatter.shortDescription: Tail-recursive call sites have no continuation
A recursive method needs to contain an if-else statement
Each block in a loop is repeated individually
The computation of all loop iterations starts from the state before the loop
In a control-flow graph, a return statement has no outgoing edge
A non-condition node in a control-flow graph can have multiple outgoing edges
super() can be called anywhere in the constructor of a subclass

expressibleInNms.how: graph of a constructor with a super() call

frontmatter.shortDescription: Exceptions get thrown at the end of the try block

expressibleInNms.how: graph of method involving try block with multiple statements

frontmatter.shortDescription: A control-flow graph node (other than the entry node) can have zero incoming edges

expressibleInNms.how: graph with nodes in addition to entry node

frontmatter.shortDescription: A control-flow graph node (other than the exit node) can have zero outgoing edges

expressibleInNms.how: graph with nodes in addition to exit node
